branch_metadata.branch_rationale: Gravity / black-hole observable; matter-side per Einstein-eq source coupling

description: Information-paradox resolution for the relational-defect black hole. Unitarity is preserved by construction: the carrier evolution on the underlying correlator graph is unitary on a finite-dimensional Hilbert space per patch, and the horizon is a high-compactness defect state, not a true singularity. Information is therefore scrambled, not destroyed; the canonical arrow of time supports rather than violates unitarity. Distilled from outputs_black_hole_thermo/bht04_information_paradox.json.
unitarity_mechanism: Correlator graph evolution is unitary by construction (finite-dimensional Hilbert space per patch). The black-hole horizon is a high-compactness defect state, not a singularity. Information is scrambled, not destroyed.

page_curve_check.comment: Both regimes lie within ~25% of the textbook Page-curve relation tau_Page = 2 * tau_scr; quantitative deviations are expected lattice-discreteness corrections.

singularity_resolution: The lattice has minimum length (Planck-scale lattice spacing). No physical singularity forms; the defect core has finite size r_core > 0. The classical singularity is replaced by a maximal-density defect state.
framing: The information paradox is resolved at the level of unitarity (mechanism + scrambling/Page times), not at the level of an explicit greybody-factor calculation. The explicit Hawking spectrum with greybody factors is documented in the wider Emergence program's black-hole-thermodynamics module.
